title: adenostyles albida subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Adenostyles albida subsp. pyrenaica, also known as White Horehound, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, soothing digestive problems, and reducing fever. Adenostyles albida subsp. pyrenaica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in salves and creams for topical applications.
